name: NIS2 EU Essential Entities Governance
rational: The NIS2 Directive (EU 2022/2555) mandates availability and resilience for essential and important entities across 18 sectors in all EU member states. This governance validates reachability of identified NIS2-regulated entities as a proxy for operational availability. It does NOT replace regulatory compliance reporting but provides continuous reachability evidence.
controls: All NIS2 essential entity nodes must respond within 800ms acceptable latency
Energy sector transmission operators must be reachable — systemic dependency
Financial market infrastructure (DORA-aligned) must achieve 95%+ availability
Digital infrastructure (IXPs, RIPE) must be reachable as foundation layer
Space sector nodes (ESA, EUSPA) included per NIS2 Annex I expansion
Public administration portals must be reachable from EU observation point
standard: EU Directive 2022/2555 (NIS2) / EU Regulation 2022/2554 (DORA) / ENISA Good Practice Guide
requirements: REQ-NIS2-001: Energy sector TSO reachability — EU-political scope
REQ-NIS2-002: Financial market regulator reachability — ECB, ESAs
REQ-NIS2-003: Digital infrastructure IXP reachability — global scope
REQ-NIS2-004: Health authority reachability — DE primary
REQ-NIS2-005: Space sector agency reachability — ESA, EUSPA, DLR
REQ-NIS2-006: Public administration portal availability — EU member states

description.purpose: NIS2 essential entity reachability validation across EU member states
description.scope: EU-political (27 members) + global for digital infrastructure
